<!-- generated by gitdocs -->
---
title: "Proxy Subsystem"
category: Architecture
last_updated: 2026-04-25
---

Overview
The Proxy Subsystem is the extension-level orchestration that ensures a local `claude-code-cache-fix` proxy is available, configured with the desired pipeline extensions, and that Claude Code sessions in VS Code route through it. It covers package discovery/installation, config sync, spawn/monitoring of the proxy process, and safe routing via `process.env.ANTHROPIC_BASE_URL`.

Components
- `extension.js`
  - Installer / discovery: `detectInstallerForPackage()`, `installPackage()`, `getPackageDir()`
  - Package inspection & patching: `patchPipelineMjsIfNeeded()`
  - Proxy config: `ensureProxyExtensionsConfig()`
  - Spawn lifecycle: `startProxyServer()`, `stopProxyServer()`, `getProxyServerPath()`
  - Env routing & migration: `computeProxyEnv()`, `applyProxyBaseUrl()`, `restoreProxyBaseUrl()`, `cleanupLegacyEnvSetting()`
  - Settings change handling and restart logic (configuration keys list)
- `package.json`
  - Declares settings, commands, and defaults that control behavior and UX (`autoStartProxy`, `autoInstallInterceptor`, `skip*` toggles, `prefixDiff`, etc.)
- File system artifacts
  - Proxy server path in package: `<packageDir>/proxy/server.mjs`
  - Proxy override extensions file: typically `~/.claude/cache-fix-proxy-extensions.json`
  - Proxy NDJSON telemetry: `~/.claude/cache-fix-proxy-log.ndjson`

Data Flow (activation → request pipeline)
1. Activation
   1. `activate()` registers configuration-change listeners and commands.
   2. `clearOurWrapperSetting()` clears legacy preload wrapper settings (v0.7.0 migration).
   3. `maybeAutoExportCerts()` exports Windows corp CA if enabled.
   4. The extension determines whether the `claude-code-cache-fix` package is present (`getPackageDir()` / `getProxyServerPath()`).
   5. If missing and `autoInstallInterceptor` is enabled, `installPackage()` runs the detected installer.
   6. If present, `patchPipelineMjsIfNeeded()` attempts an idempotent patch for Windows `import` path issues; if patched and proxy was running, it restarts the proxy.
2. Config sync
   1. `ensureProxyExtensionsConfig()` writes the proxy `extensions.json` override based on v3.1.0 defaults and per-setting opt-outs.
   2. `computeProxyEnv()` gathers env vars (HTTP(S) proxy, CA file, `CACHE_FIX_*` env toggles such as `CACHE_FIX_PREFIXDIFF`, `CACHE_FIX_SKIP_DEFERRED_TOOLS_RESTORE`) that will be passed to the proxy process.
3. Proxy spawn
   1. `startProxyServer()` locates `proxy/server.mjs` via `getProxyServerPath()`.
   2. Spawns using Node (`spawn(process.execPath, [proxyPath], { env: <merged env> })`).
   3. Writes logs and NDJSON telemetry to `~/.claude/cache-fix-proxy-log.ndjson`.
4. Runtime routing
   1. The extension sets `process.env.ANTHROPIC_BASE_URL` so VS Code's Claude Code `claude` subprocesses inherit the proxy URL.
   2. Claude Code requests hit the proxy; the proxy runs the pipeline extensions (configured by the override file) and forwards requests upstream.
5. Shutdown / disable
   1. On `disableProxy()` or `deactivate()`, `restoreProxyBaseUrl()` synchronously restores or deletes `process.env.ANTHROPIC_BASE_URL`.
   2. `stopProxyServer()` stops the child process.
   3. `cleanupLegacyEnvSetting()` removes legacy persisted `claudeCode.environmentVariables` entries that point at `127.0.0.1` (conservative cleanup).

Key Design Decisions (rationale visible in commits)
1. Drop preload/wrapper mode (v0.7.0)
   - Rationale: Preload wrapper failed on Bun-binary Claude Code; shipped Windows `.exe` enlarged VSIX. Proxy-only mode simplifies deployment and works consistently.
2. Write proxy configuration to an override file and actually wire settings to the proxy
   - Rationale: 0.6.x toggles only affected a deleted preload wrapper; v0.7.x ensures every `skip*` setting flips the corresponding extension in the proxy override, and restarts the proxy when settings change.
3. Use `process.env` for ANTHROPIC routing and avoid persisted settings (v0.7.2)
   - Rationale: Persisting `ANTHROPIC_BASE_URL` to `claudeCode.environmentVariables` could outlive the extension (async write raced with unload), leaving a stale user `settings.json` entry. `process.env` modifications are synchronous and per-extension-host (per-window) so they are reliable and don't persist to disk.
4. Conservative migration & cleanup
   - `cleanupLegacyEnvSetting()` only removes entries whose value contains `127.0.0.1` to avoid clobbering user-set corporate or custom upstream values.
5. Auto-install with toolchain awareness
   - `detectInstallerForPackage()` prefers `volta` when `npm` is managed by Volta so installs are consistent with the user's toolchain.
